title: Warm Nutty Dutch Peanut Pilaf
description: A creamy and nutty post-workout dish from the Netherlands, featuring chilled butter and a pressure-cooked pilaf, low-fat-friendly and served in a bento-box.
instructions: Rinse the rice in a fine mesh strainer until the water runs clear.
In a pressure cooker, combine the rice and water and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick-release any remaining pressure.
In a small bowl, whisk together the peanut butter, chilled butter, salt, and black pepper until smooth.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the peanut butter mixture until well combined.
Stir in the chopped peanuts and serve the pilaf hot in a bento-box.
Garnish with additional chopped peanuts if desired.
